content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Source is immutable evidence layer for re-check and explainability; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Source` is the raw knowledge source for chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, and external records; it serves as the primary evidence layer for re-check and explainability. Its design invariants emphasize no silent mutation, separate `observed_at` versus ingestion time, `content_hash` for dedupe, and an explicit delete policy.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role and invariants of `Source`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> must_not_be_mixed_with; truth status and retrieval_state are separate axes in the v3 memory data model and must not be mixed.; scope=Applies to truth status and retrieval_state semantics in the v3 memory data model document.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> governs_semantics_of; In the v3 memory data model, retrieval_state governs search participation frequency and whether an item is hot, warm, cold, archived, or hidden from ordinary retrieval.; scope=Applies to retrieval_state semantics in the v3 memory data model document.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> governs_semantics_of; In the v3 memory data model, truth status governs whether knowledge is current, superseded, refuted, or past the end of its time window.; scope=Applies to truth status semantics in the v3 memory data model document.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> must_not_change_silently; In the v3 memory data model, Source records must not be silently changed.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.1 `Source`
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> intended_for; scope_json is needed for retrieval and reconciliation in the v3 memory data model.; scope=Applies to scope fields in the v3 memory data model document.
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> has_allowed_state_set; The common retrieval lifecycle states in the v3 memory data model are active, warm, cold, archived, and deleted.; scope=Applies to retrieval lifecycle state values in the v3 memory data model document.
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Note invariants require one self-contained semantic fragment; `Note` invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md` require each note to be one self-contained semantic fragment that adds value beyond duplicating the source; `candidate_claims_json` is only hints, and a note may remain long-lived without becoming a claim if it stays useful for retrieval.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing Note invariants and lifecycle expectations.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Note represents durable intermediate semantic context; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims, such as design directions, decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining when to use `Note` and how it differs from Source, Claim, and chain-of-thought.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> erroneous_merge_must_be_reversible_via_audit_trail; In the v3 memory data model, erroneous Entity merges must be reversible through the audit trail.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.2 `Entity`
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_data_model_layer; The v3 memory data model has an evidence layer represented by Source.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> alias_points_to_current_canonical_entity; In the v3 memory data model, an Entity alias always points to the current canonical entity.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.2 `Entity`
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> can_remain_without_conversion_to_claim; In the v3 memory data model, a Note can remain long-lived without being converted into a Claim if it is useful for retrieval.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.4 Note invariants
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> status_must_update_not_duplicate; In the v3 memory data model, Task status should be updated rather than creating duplicate Task records.; scope=Applies to Task lifecycle updates in the v3 memory data model.
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> intended_for; scope_text is needed for explainability in the v3 memory data model.; scope=Applies to scope fields in the v3 memory data model document.
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> intended_for; In the v3 memory data model, Note is intended for information that is already important and useful for retrieval but should not yet be cemented as a hard fact.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.4 Note
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> must_not_be_source_final_claim_or_chain_of_thought; In the v3 memory data model, Note must not be treated as a Source, a final Claim, or chain-of-thought.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.4 Note
- claim hot-path:claim:<14> requires_explicit_delete_policy; In the v3 memory data model, Source delete policy must be explicit.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.1 `Source`
- claim hot-path:claim:<15> stores_observed_time_separately_from_ingestion_time; In the v3 memory data model, Source stores `observed_at` separately from ingestion time.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.1 `Source`
- claim hot-path:claim:<16> has_cross_cutting_concern; The v3 memory data model has retrieval lifecycle as a cross-cutting concern.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=MemoryRun; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=MemoryRun; reason=Target section explicitly discusses `MemoryRun`, which safely matches the existing memory data model concept entity.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=02. Data Model|agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md|02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=02. Data Model agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun` MARKDOWN Document title: 02. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun` Document lines: 501-539 ### 3.9 `MemoryRun` Audit trail и debug слой. Нужен, чтобы видеть: - какой prompt сработал - на какой модели - что именно было retrieved - какой budget был выделен - какие ops были предложены - какие ops реально применились - где pipeline сломался Рекомендуемые поля: - `run_id` - `namespace_id` - `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_notes | repair_memory | apply_retention | read_plan | compose_answer | compact` - `trigger_mode: hot_path | background | manual | slow_path` - `source_ids_json` - `retrieved_item_ids_json` - `retrieval_budget_json` - `prompt_name` - `prompt_version` - `model_name` - `input_hash` - `output_json` - `applied_ops_json` - `repair_actions_json` - `latency_ms` - `token_input` - `token_output` - `status: success | failed | partial` - `error_text` - `created_at` Инварианты: - run log не должен исчезать silently - `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

Document lines: 501-539

### 3.9 `MemoryRun`

Audit trail и debug слой.

Нужен, чтобы видеть:
- какой prompt сработал
- на какой модели
- что именно было retrieved
- какой budget был выделен
- какие ops были предложены
- какие ops реально применились
- где pipeline сломался

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `run_id`
- `namespace_id`
- `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_notes | repair_memory | apply_retention | read_plan | compose_answer | compact`
- `trigger_mode: hot_path | background | manual | slow_path`
- `source_ids_json`
- `retrieved_item_ids_json`
- `retrieval_budget_json`
- `prompt_name`
- `prompt_version`
- `model_name`
- `input_hash`
- `output_json`
- `applied_ops_json`
- `repair_actions_json`
- `latency_ms`
- `token_input`
- `token_output`
- `status: success | failed | partial`
- `error_text`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- run log не должен исчезать silently
- `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

- `token_output`
- `status: success | failed | partial`
- `error_text`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- run log не должен исчезать silently
- `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety
